reasoning: The FrontierMath benchmark is exceptionally difficult, with current state-of-the-art models (including Claude 3.5 Sonnet and GPT-4o) scoring below 2%. To improve from <2% to over 80% within a single year on a benchmark requiring hours or days of expert mathematician time would be an unprecedented single-year leap in AI capabilities. Even with advanced reasoning models like OpenAI o1 or future models arriving in 2025 (e.g., o2 or GPT-5), reaching an 80% success rate on unpublished, highly complex problems designed by Fields medalists and top mathematicians seems overwhelmingly unlikely for 2025. The market estimate of 3.9% reflects this long-shot probability, and I concur with a similarly low estimate.
